files.justification: Remote staged-use diagnostics centralize ordered analyzer message construction for hook-chain generation; splitting this file should happen with a dedicated diagnostics layout refactor.

Plugin server facade validation keeps cross-field contract checks together so generated server shape failures report consistent diagnostics; this merge preserves behavior until the validator is decomposed by contract area.
RPC type mapping owns the shared scalar, collection, map, and DTO classifier tables used by multiple emitters; splitting it needs a coordinated mapper API cleanup.
The JSON RPC lowerer coordinates service-handle and expression lowering state across generated fragments; existing size is accepted pending a lowerer state decomposition.
Queryable method-call translation keeps the supported method matrix and diagnostics together to avoid divergent translation behavior; split when adding a method-family registry.
Raw I32 loop expression planning is a compact decision-table implementation for compiler loop emission; split after introducing a shared raw-operation planning abstraction.

folders.justification: InvokeAsync lowering is split by receiver, capture, payload, and result-reader concerns; existing folder remains dense until the analyzer layout is reorganized.
Top-level plugin analyzer coordination contains shared readers, package generation, diagnostics, and model factories that are being split gradually by feature area.
Hook-chain analysis contains separate model, diagnostic, lowering, and support helpers; folder density is accepted until hook-chain features move under a deeper layout.
Hook-chain support helpers are intentionally separated by generated surface and ownership concern; folder density is existing analyzer organization debt.
Expression lowering is split by syntax and runtime binding concern; folder density is tolerated until a deeper lowering namespace layout is introduced.
Kernel method expression lowering uses focused files for descriptor, host-binding, and intrinsic cases that share one lowering namespace.
PluginServer facade generation is split by controls, validation, world, properties, and emit support; existing folder remains dense until the analyzer layout is reorganized.
Plugin analyzer RPC support contains client, lowerer, graft, mapper, and payload emission entry points that are still organized under one RPC feature area.
RPC payload generation is split by reader, writer, DTO, resolver, and value conversion concern while sharing mapper conventions.
Service generator models contain method, service, signature, inheritance, and return classifiers that share incremental-model contracts.
Runtime RPC support contains codecs, marshalling, DTO shape handling, and service registration helpers that share one public runtime area.
Compiler emitters are split by value, control-flow, loop, and method emission concerns but still share one low-level emission namespace.
Service client runtime helpers are split by proxy, stream, peer, and transport concerns within one public client feature area.
KernelMethod lowering has many focused regression fixtures covering distinct generated-code shapes.
Remote RunLocal runtime coverage is intentionally split into focused fixtures for local callback and projection behavior.
Kernel RPC regression coverage is split by codec, marshalling, proxy, DTO, and service-registration behavior.
Server-extension RPC regression coverage is split by lowering, DTO, fallback, payload, and runtime behavior.
